channel.title: CI/CD on Anohba Jehad
channel.description: Recent content in CI/CD on Anohba Jehad

item.title: Modern Infrastructure – Part 1: Docker
item.description: “Build once, run anywhere.” That’s not a dream - that’s Docker. What is Docker? Docker is an open-source platform that allows you to develop, ship, and run applications inside containers. Containers package your app along with its environment, dependencies, and tools - making them portable, lightweight, and consistent across all platforms. Why Docker? Traditional VMs vs Docker Feature Traditional VM Docker OS Architecture Heavy OS per VM Shared kernel, lightweight Boot Time Slower boot time Fast boot/startup Resource Usage More resources used Less CPU/RAM needed Portability Hard to move Easy to share or deploy Use Docker when you want to:

item.title: Meet Jenkins: A Guide to Automation
item.description: What is Jenkins? Jenkins is an open-source automation tool written in Java. It helps developers build, test, and deploy their software automatically, making Continuous Integration (CI) and Continuous Delivery (CD) possible. CI: Continuous Integration Automatically building and testing code whenever changes are pushed. CD: Continuous Delivery Automatically deploying/releasing the code to production or staging. Together, CI/CD makes your workflow faster, safer, and hands-free. In simple terms: Jenkins watches your code and runs jobs (like building or testing) whenever you push changes — saving you tons of manual work.
